themes: Joe Brady emotional after McDermott firing
writeup: The Buffalo Bills are generating significant buzz this June as new head coach Joe Brady opened up about the emotional toll of Sean McDermott's firing, describing himself as 'devastated' and 'broken' in the aftermath of the transition. Meanwhile, the organization is looking ahead with optimism as training camp dates have been officially announced, and fans are getting their first glimpses of bison statues going up outside the new Highmark Stadium while demolition of the old venue continues. On the roster front, outside linebacker Bradley Chubb has been making waves at minicamp, expressing his hunger to lead Buffalo to a championship and training alongside fellow pass rusher TJ Parker. With a new defensive coordinator in Jim Leonhard, key injury updates on Cole Bishop and Dorian Williams, and a fresh 53-man roster prediction circulating, the Bills appear to be in full rebuild-and-reload mode heading into the 2026 season.
